videos.title: Why Did Bitcoin Crash In 2017-2018?
videos.text: this is matthew crowder from trader university and today i want to answer the question why did bitcoin crash in 2017 if you're interested in learning how to make money in both bull and bear markets as well as seeing what i'm investing in or trading like bitcoin be sure to hit that subscribe button so we had a massive run-up in bitcoin in 2000 really in 2017. now the uh we know we have these havings every four years in bitcoin when the minor subsidy gets cut in half the uh the the last one that happened before the big run-up was in july of 2016. and we saw a very similar pattern to what we're seeing play out in where right after the the having was approximately call it here right about here in the chart and we had kind of a sideways period and nothing too spectacular and then within within uh call it 12 to 18 months we had a giant run up in bitcoin that peaked in december uh near the end of december of 2017. now these were crazy times there's a lot of icos going on a lot of other cryptocurrencies were doing really well and there's a lot of signs that sentiment had gotten completely out of hand so for example when it comes to the grayscale bitcoin investment trust which many of you know is gbtc this trust was actually trading we know that it always trades at a premium above where bitcoin itself trades but bitcoin topped out at around 20 000 per bitcoin and this trust bitcoin this trust was trading at 100 premium so you're essentially paying 40 per bitcoin plus uh obviously having to pay a two percent fee every year so this is what happened there was a big big run up in 2017 a giant crash in 2018 and the market sort of bottomed at the end of 2018 very close to the time when the u.s stock market bottomed as well after a pretty rough 2018. now it looks like we're making a new run here a lot of people are suggesting we may be in another bubble and asking me to go into what happened in 2017. now the conventional wisdom was that futures were introduced or this isn't i wouldn't say this is the conventional wisdom but this is sort of the conspiracy theory version inside the bitcoin community and outside that what happened was the cme launched bitcoin futures in december of 2017 and that somehow this enabled uh people to get short more easily before that you couldn't really short bitcoin it was very difficult to borrow it and sell it short and futures made that really easy as well as the fact that perhaps the us government was not a huge fan of bitcoin and they were able to somehow manipulate uh the price of bitcoin as they've been accused of doing and probably have been doing with the uh with the gold market using futures for a very long time but i would suggest that these while these factors may have played a part especially the fact that it was easier to short bitcoin i don't think i don't think that the bit the government at least the us government is manipulating bitcoin at this point if they are manipulating bitcoin using futures they're doing a really bad job because they have allowed it to peek back up all they needed to do if they could do it is pin it down here at uh at three or four thousand dollars per bitcoin have it stay down there forever people would go crazy and gradually lose interest but they haven't done that and it looks like bitcoin is actually out of control at this point again and i think it's very likely we see we see new highs on bitcoin later this year so i think the conventional wisdom is is uh only fractionally correct i think what really happened with bitcoin was uh much more driven by sort of it getting ahead of itself combined with tightening from the federal reserve the u.s central bank so let's go into that basically what happened is that in starting in really 2015 early 2016 the feds started raising interest rates this is a chart of the fed funds rate the effective fed funds rate which is one measurement of where interest rates are and this is set by the central bank and you can see that for uh for most of 2017 and then certainly in 2018 we had fed funds really go from about 1.2 percent all the way up to 2.4 and it turned out that this was too tight for the economy there's a lot of debt in the us economy and the global financial system and basically what happened is the fed got too tight they also tried to roll down their balance sheet this is the the central bank balance sheet the federal reserve's balance sheet where they basically print new money they buy treasuries and munis and mortgage backs and things like that now they're buying junk bonds and they stick them on their balance sheet but we can see what happened is beginning really at the end of 2017 in early 2018 the fed began to let assets roll off of its balance sheet and the way they do this if they're holding treasuries for example and the treasuries mature they just let them mature and then they don't roll them into new treasuries or they don't use the money to uh to buy new treasuries and so they were shrinking their balance sheet they were raising interest rates they're raising fed funds and this had the effect of causing a sort of mini bond bear market etc and it ended up making it very difficult for risk assets as well we can see that that for 2018 here's january 2018 the s p really chopped around quite a bit we had sort of the vol blow up in in march of 2018 we managed to make new highs and then the fourth quarter was really terrible for the s p 500 we can see that there are a lot of there's a lot of tightening that the fed was doing and they were also sort of accelerating their balance sheet the assets rolling off their balance sheet so we really had a tightening of monetary conditions that affected not just bitcoin but also risk assets like the s p 500 and uh what really what this with this combined with was bitcoin getting ahead of itself and one way to measure that is if we look at the stock to flow model this was put out by plan b on twitter if we look at this model and here's a graphic representation historical chart we can see that the big bubble was really the 2017 bubble was right here where we got very far away from the blue line which is right in the middle the blue line is sort of the model price it's the stock to flow smoothed over 463 days i'm not sure exactly why they're using this particular number you could use 360 days and get get the same the same uh value but basically what had happened is bitcoin got way ahead of itself this is why people were paying 100 over the uh a 100 premium to own bitcoin in retirement accounts and so when you have the fed be being extra tight obviously futures may be played apart as well but when you when you get so far away from the model price or where it should be trading sort of under under fair value uh there's a tendency for it to fall back to the model's value which is the blue line that's sort of obscured here and then what often happens is you'll get an overshoot and if you look at i'll link to this this uh this chart you can see that bitcoin does spend time especially right after its havings usually has these run-ups it overshoots on the upside and then it mean reverts back down to the model price and what happens is you that first 12 to 18 months of the four year cycle so 2016 to 2020 would be the uh that would be a four year cycle that having happened as we saw in july of 2016. you had this big run up at the end of 2016 and all of 2017 and then you had sort of two years of chop and something similar seems to be playing out in 2020 we had the may having in 2020 which really contributes to a shortage of supply of new bitcoin because the miners aren't being rewarded with as many and uh it looks like the price is now beginning to creep up again though we can see that it's really sort of right on the model price here it's right on the blue line it's certainly in nowhere in bubble territory as it was back here and what's very interesting is if we look at this model we can see we're right here call it july 30th 2020. the stock to flow price is eleven thousand nine hundred twenty the model price and we're currently at about eleven thousand one hundred but you can see as the time progresses the model price moves up quite sharply now we don't know if this model will hold but if it does this suggests that uh bitcoin if it's going to be at the model price should be trading at at all time new highs by the end of 2020 uh it should be around 25 000 and then this really just continues through 2021 and by this time call it roughly this time next summer in 2021 maybe by september 2021 when school starts you're very close to 99 000 or 100 000. so what i'm expecting is i'm expecting something similar to what happened back in 2017 i'm expecting uh the the price to continue to appreciate we'll probably get in an overshoot as we did the price will go way above the model price and then we'll see what happens there's a good chance that it does not mean revert in this case simply because plan b's model is around now was not around in 2017 and so there's a chance that especially institutional investors start to front run this model and price in the 2024 having and the 2028 having the prices associated with each of those are roughly if i remember correctly 300 or 400 000 per bitcoin for the 2024 having and i believe it's three million dollars for the 2028 having now obviously bitcoin can do what it wants models don't work forever but this is what the model is suggesting it's suggesting that we're certainly not in bubble territory here we're right kind of right where the oops just destroyed the chart we're right where the the model suggests it should be and uh we're about to enter into the most bullish 12 to 18 period 12 to 18 month period for bitcoin uh so that's that's where i think we are now i'll link to this chart maybe you can do a better job of not screwing it up like i'm doing here and you can check it out for yourself but i think what's very different now is that we are in a a point of the of sort of fed monetary policy where they have interest rates at zero they may go negative in fact we have the fed growing its balance sheet i really at an enormous rate growing the money supply growing the balance sheet from about four trillion to uh the high six almost seven trillion at the peak a little bit above seven trillion at the peak and so we have these very loose monetary conditions the fed is printing like mad and i think this is very very bullish for bitcoin and uh there will be people who had bad experiences back in 2017 and uh and you know will remember uh we'll remember what happened and it's very unlikely that anyone except very hardcore uh bitcoin uh bitcoiners are still holding from this peak most of people probably who didn't have high conviction and didn't really believe in bitcoin capitulated uh but what we do know is once we get to new highs here once we get above 20k anyone who's ever bought bitcoin anyone who's ever held it for whatever period of time will be uh will have a profit on their position or they'll at least be break even anything above 20k they'll they'll have a profit obviously these guys who are in in uh bought grayscale at 100 premium they won't but anyone who's holding the underlying bitcoin will have a uh a profit at least on paper and this is when the fireworks will really start and uh presumably this is later this year as that model price creeps up to around 25 000 and bitcoin itself is drawn up to the model price both by just the model price being a good mathematical representation of how these things work as well as the needs of the fed to just continue to print more money they're going to have to continue to print more money they're going to have to send out checks to everyone additional unemployment benefits especially going in an election year it makes a lot of sense that the fed will continue to print and have very loose monetary policy this is one reason that uh gold and silver are behaving so well as sort of precious metals or monetary metals and i think it's another reason that bitcoin is really waking up here again we're in the most bullish part of the cycle from now until december of 2021 so i'm long bitcoin i have a very large position in it and i'm happy to be in bitcoin if you found this video helpful uh let me know your questions comments and please hit that subscribe and like button if you found the video helpful thanks a lot for listening and i'll see in the next video

videos.title: WHY does bitcoin�s price crash after a huge run up, like 2011, 2013, 2017, and 2021?
videos.text: Why does Bitcoin's price crash after a huge run up like 20 20 uh 2011, 2013, 2017, and 2021? Well, in all of those cases, Bitcoin is an amazing asset, so it goes up over time. As the price goes up, more people notice that the price is going up. Eventually, lots of people notice that the price is going up, and people get riskier and riskier with how they're buying Bitcoin. So initially when the price is down at a normal sustainable level based on uh based on adoption of Bitcoin, people are doing very normal things with it. They're buying it with money they actually have. But as they get more confident that the price is going up and it's going to keep going up and it's going up even more and more and more, people start getting risky. So they start doing increasingly uh more unsustainable things to buy Bitcoin and eventually they start borrowing money to buy Bitcoin and then they borrow money against borrowed money to buy Bitcoin and then they borrow against their Bitcoin to get more money to buy more Bitcoin. And all of those things involve having to pay back the money money that was borrowed, which means they're only sustainable if the price keeps going up. And the more the price goes up, the riskier people get. And pretty soon they're betting it's going to go up 2 to1. then they're betting it's going to go up 5 to one, then 10 to one, then 40 to one. And even some websites I I believe like Bitmax allow you to bet 100 to one on your money that something is going up. So the the higher it gets, the more people get crazier and crazier with how they're buying it. And eventually the person who is borrowing money 100 to1, 40 to1, even 10 to1 has to pay that money back or the market just can't sustain the upward trajectory because there's just not enough new adoption of Bitcoin to sustain that parabolic rise. As a result, the price starts to fall. Well, as the price starts to fall, everyone who borrowed money to buy it, which made the price go up at the very end of the parabolic rise, parabolic means parabola, meaning it's like a line that basically starts going up and then up and up and up and then goes basically vertical up. That's called a uh parabolic rise. Well, that happens because people are getting riskier and riskier. Now, long-term, the market can actually afford almost any price level of Bitcoin. So, for example, the price topped out in 2011 at $32 before going through a major correction down to $2. Now, did that mean Bitcoin was not worth $32? No. It meant that back in 2011 when the total number of people using Bitcoin was tiny, tiny, tiny, it could not sustain $32, which is why it went back down to $2. Again, that didn't mean it wasn't going to be worth $32. It just meant that the adoption was not quite ready to sustain a $32 price. Same when the price went up to $1,200, that did not mean Bitcoin was not worth $1,200. It just meant that the marketplace it went up so fast from $2 up to $1,200 that it was there was not enough market uh adoption at that time. I mean, we're dealing with like total market worldwide adoption of Bitcoin back in 2013 of like I don't know.1%. I mean, just ridiculously small numbers of people had adopted Bitcoin, which meant $1,200. Bitcoin was not quite ready to sustain a $1,200 price because it got there in the case of $32 and the case of $1,200. It got there over a series of months where the price just went crazy up. And it went crazy up and people started speculating and they started speculating and people piled in who had no idea what Bitcoin was. They just loved the fact that it seemed to be going up. And so it went up and up and up and then it became unsustainable. So the same is true in 2017. It's not that Bitcoin was not worth $19,000. It just was not worth 19. It couldn't sustain that price in 2017. And same in 2021 at $69,000. Obviously, Bitcoin is worth more than that today. And even the biggest price drop this year back in April, I think it was like April 5th or whatever it was. Anyway, sometime in April when it dropped back to $74,000, well, that's $74,000 after a 33% price correction, that is still $5,000 higher than the highest it ever got in 2021. So, even in a major dip, it was still higher than the than the peak of the 2021 price, which tells us again that Bitcoin was worth more than $69,000. it just was not ready to sustain that price level in 2021 because the adoption in 2021 was not quite high enough to sustain a price that high. So the same is true today. Now I personally do not think $114,000 is overheated. Again, you can follow James Czech. He has by far the best research on uh Bitcoin and whether it's overheated or not overheated and whether there's likely to be a price correction and if so, how much. Um, and usually I post uh some of his stuff. Sometimes I'll post some highlights. But it cost $29 a month. But if you're super into Bitcoin or if you're planning on buying a lot of Bitcoin over the coming months, it is absolutely worth $29 a month. Even if you just turn it on, read a few updates and turn it off or turn it on for a month or two and then turn it off. Um, but anyway, he's fantastic and he goes through why why we are in the cycles, if we're overheated, how much people are borrowing, using borrowed money to buy Bitcoin. He he walks through all of those metrics um and explains them to you in a pretty accessible way to help you understand are we in the parabolic phase when things are going crazy or are we still in the relative calm of you know the seventh eighth or ninth inning of this thing. So um but anyway all assets work that way. So, Amazon, back after the dot crash, the price of Amazon dropped from $106 to, I believe, $6. It was a 94% drop, comparable to Bitcoin's price crash from $32 down to $2. So, 32 down to two is the same as 106 down to six. So, uh, right after the.com bust in 2020, sorry, not 2020, uh, the year 2000, the price correction of Amazon stock was roughly equivalent to the very first big price correction uh, that Bitcoin went through. Uh, does that mean Amazon stock was not worth $106? And again, it's gone through some stock splits. So, it's, you know, a lot higher than that now. And it's even a lot higher than whatever the stock chart shows right now as compared to the past because I believe it went through some stock splits. So that one that 106 down to six uh is probably comparatively different numbers now. Uh but anyway, so Amazon is radically higher radically radically radically higher than the prices back in the year 2000 because again I don't know what maybe the equivalent is like it went from like 160 cents to 6 cents or something like that. Anyway, whatever it was, uh, it's been through a bunch of stock splits, which means you can't really compare current numbers to the past unless you compare it on current stock charts, which I have not done. But anyway, that does not mean Amazon was not worth the price in 20 uh 2001. it was it just wasn't quite ready to sustain that price right then because the total adoption uh worldwide of Amazon and um online you know e-commerce web you know purchasing uh was not quite there yet would it become there yes absolutely ultimately Amazon is now one of the most valuable companies in the world it just wasn't quite ready to sustain that back then but it got there it grew into it so another way to think about it is you know is Bitcoin worth $32? Yes, it just needed to grow into that. Was it worth $1,200? Yes, it just needed to grow grow into that. Was it worth $19,000? Yes, it needed to grow into that. Was it worth 69,000? Yes, it needed to grow into that. Um, so if we get a huge price rise in the second half of this year, for example, we shoot from $114,000 up to 180 or 210 or 220 or 236, which is like the highest it could possibly go uh this year, which is like highly unlikely, but could go, who knows? Uh, if we go that high, there will be a price correction because it's not that Bitcoin is not worth that. It's because you got to take some time to grow into things. uh just like a kid, you know, and they're closed. If the, you know, if if you buy an, you know, if you do buy an adult or a uh, you know, a youth large for your kid and it's way too big for them, that doesn't mean it's always going to be too big for them. It just means they're going to have to grow into that youth large and they may only really be a youth medium or a youth small. They will become a youth large and then they will become ultimately adult sizes. It's just going to take them some time. So to recap, Bitcoin goes through price corrections because there's a huge parabolic rise before them. If Bitcoin does not go through a huge parabolic rise, that tends to result in not having huge price corrections. Um that again, that doesn't mean you can't get a 33% or 32% price correction. We have had two price corrections between 30% and 33% uh since 2022. Um, so if that's absolutely possible on the way up, but that's obviously different than the massive price corrections that were experienced in the early days of Bitcoin. So hopefully that makes sense. As James Czech says, the, you know, the bull authors the bear if there's a huge price rise that's unsustainable. There's got to be a price correction as the market grows into that value. If the price went up that high because a ton of people borrowed a ton of money, well, guess what? They're going to have to pay back that money. and the price is going to have to come back down to adjust. Um, so I don't think we are in a parabolic rise right now. It does not feel like we are, but you never know. And James Czech is by far the best person to follow to give insights on where we are in the cycle if we're at the top. And probably the best thing you can do with James Czech is not buy the very tip top of the market because he will he will tell you when things are getting steamy and overcooked and you know uh the price is unsustainable and it just has to come back down because all the metrics he's looking at tell you exactly that. Hopefully all that makes sense. Hopefully it's helpful. Thanks everyone.

videos.title: Don't Buy Bitcoin. It's Going To Crash!!!
videos.text: This one time, bitcoin went from 6 cents all the way to 36 cents and then it crashed down to 21 cents and another time bitcoin went from 85 cents all the way to 29 dollars, and then it crashed all the way to 3 dollars. and then another time bitcoin all the way to $213 and then it crashed all the way to $70 And then another time... bitcoin went all the way to $1100 and then crashed all the way to $239. So the moral on this story is: don't buy bitcoins. Because you know it's gonna crash again.
